sets.passage: As artificial intelligence systems assume responsibilities once reserved for humans, the ethical questions they raise grow increasingly (1)____. A hiring algorithm that quietly favours certain candidates can perpetuate the very prejudices its designers hoped to (2)____. Because such systems learn from historical data, they tend to (3)____ patterns of discrimination that society has spent decades trying to overcome, and they do so at a scale no individual recruiter ever could. The trouble is that their reasoning often remains (4)____ even to the engineers who built them, making accountability difficult to assign. When a self-driving car must choose between two harmful outcomes, who bears the moral (5)____ for its decision, the owner, the manufacturer, or the lines of code itself? Critics argue that companies have been far too (6)____ to deploy powerful tools without first weighing their consequences for ordinary lives. They call for independent bodies to (7)____ how these systems behave once released into the messy real world, rather than trusting glossy corporate assurances. Others worry that excessive regulation could (8)____ the very innovation that makes the technology valuable in the first place. A sensible path forward demands that developers build transparency into their products from the outset, so that decisions can later be (9)____ and challenged by those affected. Equally important is broad public debate, for the values embedded in code ultimately reflect the priorities of those who write it. Ethics, in this context, cannot be an afterthought (10)____ onto a finished product; it must guide every stage of design. Only by treating fairness as a core requirement, not a luxury, can engineers earn the trust on which the future of intelligent machines truly depends.
sets.explain_zh: (1)acute：形容词作表语，指伦理问题日益“尖锐/严峻”，与increasingly搭配自然。(2)eliminate：动词不定式宾语，设计者希望“消除”偏见，hoped to后接动词原形。(3)reinforce：动词原形，系统“强化”了歧视模式，tend to后接原形且语义为加强。(4)opaque：形容词作表语，推理对工程师仍是“不透明的”，与后文accountability难追责呼应。(5)responsibility：名词，承担道德“责任”，moral responsibility固定搭配。(6)eager：形容词，公司“急于”部署，too...to结构表过度。(7)scrutinise：动词原形，独立机构“审查”系统行为。(8)stifle：动词原形，过度监管可能“扼杀”创新。(9)examined：过去分词作被动，决策能被“审视”并质疑，与challenged并列。(10)grafted：过去分词，伦理不能是“嫁接/附加”到成品上的事后补充，grafted onto搭配。干扰项：convenient(便利的)语义不合任何空；fragile(脆弱的)无对应槽位；decline(下降/拒绝)语义和搭配均不符；abundant(丰富的)不合伦理问题语境；narrow(狭窄的)无合适空位，且与各空POS/语义不匹配。

sets.passage: Conservationists warn that the planet is losing species at a rate that has no (1)____ in recorded history, a wave of extinction unfolding within a single human lifetime. Each plant and animal occupies a particular niche, and the disappearance of even one can (2)____ an entire food web, triggering collapses far from where the first loss occurred. Tropical rainforests, which (3)____ a staggering share of the world's biodiversity, are being cleared faster than scientists can catalogue what lives within them. Once a forest is gone, the (4)____ knowledge of the communities who depended on it for medicine and food often vanishes too. Yet the value of biodiversity is not merely sentimental; healthy ecosystems quietly (5)____ services such as pollination and water purification on which agriculture everywhere relies. Protecting these systems requires more than fencing off (6)____ reserves and hoping that nature will somehow recover on its own. Effective strategies must (7)____ local people, whose cooperation ultimately determines whether laws are obeyed or quietly ignored. Where communities share directly in the benefits of conservation, poaching tends to (8)____ sharply within a few seasons. Some governments have begun to reward landowners who keep habitats (9)____ rather than converting them to farmland or pasture. Such incentives recognise that the long-term (10)____ of preserving a wetland or coral reef may far outweigh the short-term profit of destroying it. Saving biodiversity, then, is not a battle against development but a smarter and more patient form of it, one that carefully counts the true worth of the living world before it is too late to act on what little still remains.
sets.explain_zh: (1)parallel：名词，指物种消失速率在历史上“没有先例/可比者”，no parallel固定表达。(2)destabilise：动词原形，单一物种消失会“破坏”整个食物网的稳定。(3)harbour：动词，雨林“蕴藏/容纳”大量生物多样性，主语which指forests故用原形。(4)intricate：形容词修饰knowledge，指“错综复杂的”本土知识。(5)deliver：动词原形，生态系统“提供”授粉、净水等服务。(6)remote：形容词，圈起“偏远的”保护区。(7)engage：动词原形，策略须“调动/吸纳”当地人。(8)diminish：动词原形，偷猎倾向于“减少”，tends to后接原形。(9)intact：形容词作宾补，keep habitats intact保持栖息地“完好”。(10)dividend：名词，保护的长期“回报”超过短期利润。干扰项：verbose(冗长的)仅修饰言辞文本，‘fencing off verbose reserves’与‘verbose knowledge’均语义荒谬，不填任何空；expand(扩张)与各处语义相反；deceptive(欺骗性的)无对应；lukewarm(不冷不热的/冷淡的)修饰态度或液体，‘lukewarm reserves’不成立，不与remote争(6)；scarce(稀缺的)POS与intact槽位语义不符，均无法填入任一空。

sets.passage: The sudden and largely unplanned shift to working from home has reshaped how teams cooperate across distance, compressing decades of expected change into a few hectic months. Where colleagues once gathered around a whiteboard, they now (1)____ on shared documents that update in real time for everyone at once. This new arrangement offers obvious (2)____: workers reclaim hours once lost to commuting and can structure their days with far greater (3)____ than any office ever allowed. Yet remote collaboration also carries hidden costs that surface only gradually. The casual conversations that once sparked ideas in corridors have largely (4)____, and managers struggle to read the subtle cues that signal when a teammate is quietly overwhelmed. To compensate, many organisations have adopted tools that (5)____ progress and keep distributed members aligned around common goals. But software alone cannot (6)____ the trust that grows slowly from shared experience and small daily kindnesses. The most successful teams therefore deliberately (7)____ moments for informal contact, recognising that genuine connection rarely happens by accident online. They also resist the temptation to schedule meetings that could just as easily be a brief written message, since an (8)____ calendar drains energy without advancing the work at all. Crucially, leaders must learn to judge employees by what they actually (9)____ rather than by the hours they appear to be online, a change that demands real (10)____ in how performance is measured and rewarded. Done thoughtfully and with genuine care, distributed work can prove far more humane than the crowded office it replaces; done carelessly, it merely scatters the same old frustrations across a dozen lonely kitchen tables.
sets.explain_zh: (1)collaborate：动词原形，同事如今“协作”于共享文档，collaborate on搭配且有on。(2)advantages：名词复数，远程提供明显“优势”，后接冒号列举。(3)flexibility：名词，以更大的“灵活性”安排日程，greater flexibility搭配。(4)evaporated：过去分词，激发灵感的闲聊大多“消失了”，have largely后接过去分词。(5)track：动词原形，工具“追踪”进度。(6)manufacture：动词原形，软件无法“制造/凭空生产”信任，语义独特。(7)carve：动词原形，主动“开辟”非正式接触时刻，carve moments。(8)overloaded：过去分词作定语，“超载的”日程耗能。(9)produce：动词，按员工“产出”评判，what they produce。(10)rethinking：名词/动名词，要求在绩效衡量上真正“重新思考”，real rethinking。干扰项：remote(远程的)虽与主题相关但无空可填(各槽POS不符)；decline(下降)语义不合；abundant(丰富的)无对应；summon(召唤)与carve竞争但搭配/语义不合moments for contact；tidy(整洁的)与overloaded争(8)却语义相反——整洁的日历不会drains energy，且(8)前文是‘会议本可一条消息解决’指向超载，故唯overloaded贴切，均失配。

sets.passage: Shoppers increasingly say they want to buy responsibly, yet their behaviour at the till often (1)____ those good intentions in the most ordinary ways. Faced with a cheaper but less sustainable option, many quietly (2)____ on their principles the moment a price tag comes into view. Researchers call this gap between stated attitude and actual action a persistent (3)____ that marketers have only begun to understand. Part of the explanation is that the environmental harm of a purchase feels (4)____, distant in both time and place, while the saving is immediate and concrete in the hand. Brands eager to appear green sometimes (5)____ their credentials, a deceptive practice that erodes trust completely once it is exposed. To shift habits genuinely, companies must make the sustainable choice the (6)____ one, so that doing right requires no extra effort or research from the buyer. Clear labelling helps consumers (7)____ products whose claims are genuine from those that are mere marketing dressed up as virtue. Price, too, remains decisive; until durable goods stop being more expensive than disposable ones, many households will continue to (8)____ the throwaway option without guilt. Some economists therefore argue that taxes should (9)____ the hidden cost of pollution into the price tag, nudging buyers toward better decisions without lecturing them. Behavioural studies repeatedly show that small changes in how options are arranged on a shelf can sway far more shoppers than any earnest appeal to their conscience ever manages to. Ultimately, lasting change depends much less on guilt than on careful design: when the responsible path is also the easy and affordable one, sustainable consumption quietly stops being a sacrifice and instead becomes the (10)____.
sets.explain_zh: (1)betrays：动词第三人称单数，行为常“背叛/辜负”良好意图，主语behaviour单数。(2)compromise：动词原形，对原则“妥协”，compromise on搭配且有on。(3)paradox：名词，态度与行动差距是持久的“悖论”，a persistent paradox。(4)abstract：形容词作表语，环境危害感觉“抽象”，与distant并列。(5)exaggerate：动词原形，品牌“夸大”其环保资质，deceptive practice呼应。(6)default：名词/形容词作宾补，使可持续选择成为“默认”选项，the default one。(7)distinguish：动词原形，帮助消费者“区分”真伪，distinguish...from。(8)favour：动词原形，家庭继续“偏好”一次性选项，favour the throwaway option。(9)incorporate：动词原形，税收应把隐藏成本“纳入”价格，incorporate...into。(10)norm：名词，可持续消费悄然成为“常态”，the norm。干扰项：remote(遥远的)与abstract争(4)但remote不修饰harm的感受且搭配不当；decline(下降)语义不合；vivid(生动的)与abstract反义不能填(4)；sincere(真诚的)语义近genuine却POS/槽位无处可放；reduce(减少)与incorporate争(9)但reduce...into不成搭配，均失配。

sets.passage: Across many countries, the cost of a university degree has climbed so steeply that graduates now begin their working lives already (1)____ by debt before earning a single salary. For some, monthly repayments (2)____ a significant share of every paycheque, delaying the milestones that once defined adult life. Young people postpone buying homes, starting families, or launching businesses, not from any lack of ambition but from sheer financial (3)____ they did not choose. Defenders of the system argue that a degree remains a sound (4)____, since educated workers tend to earn considerably more over a lifetime. Critics counter that this comforting logic conveniently (5)____ the experience of those who borrow heavily yet enter fields where salaries stay stubbornly low. The burden falls (6)____ on students from poorer backgrounds, who must borrow more and can rely on no family cushion when repayment grows difficult. Some economists fear that a whole generation weighed down by such obligations may eventually (7)____ the wider economy, since money spent servicing loans cannot circulate as ordinary consumer demand. Proposals to ease the strain range from forgiving certain debts to making tuition entirely (8)____ for low-income applicants who qualify. Whatever remedy finally prevails, few serious observers dispute that the present arrangement has grown plainly (9)____. Universities, for their part, insist that rising fees merely reflect the genuine cost of teaching and research, while critics suspect that easy lending has simply allowed prices to drift upward unchecked. A society that loudly urges its young to study, and then quietly (10)____ them for doing exactly that, sends a deeply contradictory message it can ill afford to keep repeating for another anxious generation already wary of the promises made to it.
sets.explain_zh: (1)burdened：过去分词，毕业生职业生涯一开始就“背负”债务，begin...burdened by。(2)consume：动词原形，月供“吞噬”每份薪水的很大一部分。(3)constraint：名词，纯粹出于财务“约束”，financial constraint。(4)investment：名词，学位仍是稳妥的“投资”，a sound investment。(5)ignores：动词第三人称单数，此逻辑“忽视”了重负借款者的经历，主语logic单数。(6)disproportionately：副词，负担“不成比例地”落在穷困学生身上。(7)drag：动词原形，背债一代可能“拖累”更广经济，drag the economy。(8)free：形容词作宾补，使学费对低收入申请者“免费”，make tuition free。(9)unsustainable：形容词作表语，现行安排已变得“不可持续”。(10)penalises：动词第三人称单数，社会随后“惩罚”年轻人求学，主语society单数。干扰项：remote(遥远的)无对应空；abundant(丰富的)语义不合；decline(下降)与各动词槽搭配不符；rigid(僵硬的)无合适语义位；vivid(生动的)与各空POS/语义均不匹配，五项对每空均失败。

sets.passage: Modern medicine has long been celebrated for its dramatic cures, yet its quietest achievements lie in prevention rather than rescue. Vaccination campaigns and clean water have (1)____ more lives than any operating theatre ever could, though such victories rarely make headlines or win awards. The underlying logic of prevention is simple: it is far cheaper to stop a disease early than to (2)____ its consequences once it has taken firm hold. Despite this, health budgets the world over remain stubbornly (3)____ toward treatment, partly because a crisis quietly averted leaves nothing visible to celebrate. Encouraging people to adopt healthier habits is much harder than it sounds, for the rewards of regular exercise or a balanced diet are (4)____ rather than instant. Public campaigns therefore aim to make the healthy choice the convenient one, redesigning everyday environments so that good behaviour requires less (5)____. Yet prevention is not merely a matter of individual resolve; many of the strongest (6)____ of health, such as housing and income, lie far beyond any doctor's reach. Tackling them properly demands that health authorities (7)____ with sectors as varied as education and urban planning. When an outbreak does occur, a well-prepared system can swiftly (8)____ it before it spreads, sparing whole communities enormous suffering. Investing in prevention thus yields a (9)____ that compounds quietly over decades, even if no single politician can ever claim the credit. History records the surgeons who pull patients back from the brink, yet seldom the officials whose dull decisions ensured that thousands never fell ill at all. The deepest challenge is therefore to learn to value the quiet catastrophes that never happen as highly as the dramatic rescues we can plainly (10)____.
sets.explain_zh: (1)saved：过去分词，疫苗与清洁水“拯救”了更多生命，have...saved。(2)reverse：动词原形，疾病一旦发作很难“扭转”其后果。(3)skewed：过去分词作表语，预算仍“偏向”治疗，skewed toward。(4)gradual：形容词作表语，运动饮食的回报是“渐进的”而非即时，与instant对照。(5)willpower：名词，使良好行为需要更少“意志力”，requires less willpower。(6)determinants：名词复数，健康的最强“决定因素”如住房收入。(7)coordinate：动词原形，卫生部门须与各部门“协调”，coordinate with有with。(8)contain：动词原形，系统能在扩散前“遏制”疫情，contain it。(9)return：名词，投资带来数十年复利的“回报”，a return that compounds。(10)witness：动词原形，与我们能“目睹”的救援并列，rescues we can witness。干扰项：remote(遥远的)无空；abundant(丰富的)语义不合预算或回报；decline(下降)与各槽搭配不符；rigid(僵硬的)与skewed争(3)但预算应为偏向而非僵硬且rigid toward不成搭配；vivid(生动的)与gradual反向不合(4)，均无法正确填入。

sets.passage: Few technologies have rewired daily life as quickly as social networks, and few have provoked such fierce debate about their lasting (1)____ on the way we think and feel. Designed above all to keep users scrolling, their algorithms quietly (2)____ whatever content holds attention longest, which is often the most outrageous, fearful, or divisive. As a result, users may find themselves trapped in a (3)____ where every post confirms what they already believe and dissenting voices simply fade away. This steady narrowing of perspective can (4)____ political divisions, turning reasonable disagreements into bitter and lasting hostility. The platforms also exert a subtle pressure on the young, whose fragile sense of self-worth becomes (5)____ to the tally of likes a single photograph happens to attract. Defenders rightly point out that these same networks let scattered communities (6)____ across continents and give a voice to people history once ignored entirely. The truth is that the technology is neither wholly good nor wholly bad; its effects depend heavily on how it is (7)____ and by whom. Critics increasingly demand that companies be made (8)____ for the harm their products cause, much as carmakers must answer for faulty brakes. Others place their hopes instead in users themselves becoming far more (9)____ consumers of everything that appears in their crowded feeds. Regulators in several countries have already begun drafting rules that would force the largest platforms to open their systems to outside inspection. What seems certain, whatever happens next, is that a tool capable of quietly reshaping how billions of people think and vote cannot remain (10)____ from meaningful public oversight forever.
sets.explain_zh: (1)influence：名词，关于其持久“影响”的激烈辩论，about their influence。(2)amplify：动词原形，算法“放大”最吸引注意的内容。(3)bubble：名词，用户困于“信息茧房/泡泡”，a bubble where。(4)deepen：动词原形，视角窄化会“加深”政治分歧。(5)tethered：过去分词作表语，自我价值被“拴系”于点赞数，tethered to有to。(6)connect：动词原形，分散社群得以跨洲“连接”，let...connect。(7)wielded：过去分词作被动，效果取决于技术如何被“运用”及被谁运用，how it is wielded。(8)accountable：形容词作宾补，公司被要求为危害“负责”，made accountable for。(9)discerning：形容词，用户成为更有“辨别力的”消费者。(10)exempt：形容词作表语，工具不能永远“免受/豁免于”公众监督，exempt from有from，且全文逻辑是‘必须接受监督’故唯exempt贴切。干扰项：costly(昂贵的)语义不合任何空，且与exempt无近义冲突；abundant(丰富的)无空；decline(下降)搭配不符；rigid(僵硬的)无合适语义位；vivid(生动的)与discerning争(9)但vivid不修饰consumers，均失配。

sets.passage: Cities around the world are rethinking how their inhabitants move, conscious that the car-centred model of the last century has clearly reached its (1)____. Congested roads and polluted air have prompted planners to (2)____ space away from private vehicles and toward buses, bicycles, and pedestrians. The vision now steadily gaining ground is the so-called compact city, in which homes, shops, and offices sit close enough to (3)____ the need for long daily journeys altogether. Electric vehicles, though far cleaner than their predecessors, do little to ease the congestion that (4)____ urban life, since a battery-powered car still occupies the same scarce strip of road. Far more promising are dense networks of trains and trams that can move large numbers of people (5)____, leaving the streets quieter, cleaner, and safer for everyone. Some cities have already begun to (6)____ drivers a charge for entering crowded centres, using the revenue to improve the alternatives. Such measures often meet stiff resistance at first, yet residents tend to (7)____ them readily once the everyday benefits become visible. The hardest task is to ensure that the new systems serve everyone, not only the (8)____ few who can afford to live downtown. Transport, after all, determines who can reach a job, a clinic, or a school, and a poorly designed network can quietly (9)____ inequality for generations. Cities that have already redesigned their streets around people rather than vehicles report quieter neighbourhoods, livelier high streets, and children once again playing where traffic used to dominate. The future that planners now (10)____ is one where mobility is abundant and clean, yet the private car, at last, is merely one convenient option among many.
sets.explain_zh: (1)limit：名词，以汽车为中心的模式已达“极限”，reached its limit。(2)reallocate：动词原形，规划者把空间“重新分配”离开私家车，reallocate space away from。(3)eliminate：动词原形，紧凑城市“消除”长途出行的需要，eliminate the need。(4)plagues：动词第三人称单数，拥堵“困扰”城市生活，主语congestion单数。(5)efficiently：副词，列车电车“高效地”运送大量人。(6)levy：动词原形，城市开始向司机“征收”进城费，levy drivers a charge。(7)embrace：动词原形，居民往往“接纳”这些措施，tend to后接原形。(8)privileged：形容词，不仅服务“特权的”少数，the privileged few。(9)entrench：动词原形，糟糕网络会“固化”不平等。(10)envisage：动词原形，主语planners复数，规划者“设想”的未来，that planners envisage。干扰项：remote(遥远的)无对应空；decline(下降)搭配不符；rigid(僵硬的)无合适语义位；vivid(生动的)与各空POS不合；sincere(真诚的)语义和槽位均不匹配，五项对每空皆失败。

sets.passage: Museums and archives are often imagined as dusty repositories of a forgotten past, yet their true purpose is to keep human memory (1)____ and accessible to all who come looking. Behind every public gallery lies a vast hidden collection that curators must carefully (2)____, deciding what to preserve when both storage and money are strictly finite. Their choices are never neutral; what a society chooses to keep, and what it quietly allows to (3)____, reveals a great deal about its real values. For centuries many institutions filled their cabinets with treasures (4)____ from distant lands, a troubling legacy that now prompts urgent debates about ownership and return. Digitisation promises to (5)____ access dramatically, letting a student in one country examine a fragile manuscript held in another without ever boarding a plane. Yet a scanned image can never fully (6)____ the texture of an ancient page, and screens may never quite replace the quiet awe of standing before an original. Archivists also face the strange paradox that the digital records of today are surprisingly (7)____; formats grow obsolete and files corrupt far faster than old parchment ever decays. To remain genuinely relevant, museums increasingly (8)____ their communities, inviting them to help interpret objects rather than merely admire them from behind glass. In doing so, these institutions become less a (9)____ of dead relics and more a living forum for argument and discovery. Every generation inherits only what its predecessors thought worth saving, and tomorrow's historians will study us through the fragments we choose to keep today. Their enduring task, then, is to ensure that the ordinary stories of the present are (10)____ just as faithfully as those of the distant past.
sets.explain_zh: (1)alive：形容词作宾补，使人类记忆保持“鲜活”并可获取，keep memory alive。(2)curate：动词原形，策展人须小心“策划/管理”藏品。(3)perish：动词原形，社会允许什么“消亡”，allows it to perish。(4)looted：过去分词作定语，从远方“掠夺来的”珍宝，treasures looted from。(5)broaden：动词原形，数字化“拓宽”获取渠道，broaden access。(6)convey：动词原形，扫描图像无法完全“传达”古页质感。(7)fragile：形容词作表语，今日数字记录意外地“脆弱”，与后文格式过时呼应。(8)consult：动词原形，博物馆日益“咨询/征询”社区意见。(9)mausoleum：名词，机构不再是遗物的“陵墓”，与living forum对照。(10)recorded：过去分词被动，确保当下故事被忠实“记录”，are recorded。干扰项：remote(遥远的)无对应空；decline(下降)与perish争(3)但decline语义为‘衰退’不合allows it to且不及perish贴切；rigid(僵硬的)与fragile争(7)但记录应为脆弱非僵硬，rigid不合files corrupt逻辑；tedious(乏味的)语义为‘冗长无聊’，‘keep memory tedious and accessible’语义荒谬，不合(1)且不合任何形容词槽位；sincere(真诚的)语义槽位均不匹配，五项失配。

sets.passage: Scientists have always struggled to explain their work to the wider public, but the stakes of getting it right have rarely been higher than they are today. When findings about climate or disease are poorly communicated, confusion and mistrust quickly (1)____ in the vacuum that careful explanation should have filled. Part of the difficulty is that the careful, hedged language of research sits awkwardly beside a media culture that craves bold, (2)____ headlines above all else. A study suggesting only a tentative link is easily (3)____ into a confident promise of a cure, and the inevitable correction rarely receives anything like equal attention. Good science communicators therefore learn to (4)____ complex ideas without distorting them, a delicate balance far harder to strike than it first appears. They firmly resist the urge to (5)____ uncertainty, knowing that honesty about what remains unknown ultimately builds rather than erodes lasting credibility. A striking metaphor can illuminate a difficult concept, yet a careless one may (6)____ a misconception that then takes years of patient work to undo. Trust, moreover, depends as much on who delivers a message as on its actual content; audiences are far more likely to (7)____ information from someone who plainly shares their concerns. The rise of social media has made every researcher a potential broadcaster, an opportunity that is also a genuine (8)____. A single viral post can spread an error to millions long before any expert can (9)____ it. The ultimate goal is not to win arguments but to equip ordinary citizens to weigh evidence for themselves and reach (10)____ conclusions.
sets.explain_zh: (1)rush：动词原形，困惑与不信任迅速“涌入”真空，rush in the vacuum。(2)sensational：形容词修饰headlines，渴望大胆“耸动的”标题。(3)inflated：过去分词被动，试探性关联易被“夸大”为确定承诺，is inflated into。(4)simplify：动词原形，传播者学会“简化”复杂观点而不扭曲。(5)conceal：动词原形，抵制“隐瞒”不确定性的冲动。(6)implant：动词原形，草率比喻可能“植入”一个误解，implant a misconception。(7)trust：动词原形，受众更可能“信任”来自同道者的信息，likely to后接原形。(8)hazard：名词，机会也是一种真正的“危险”，also a hazard。(9)rebut：动词原形，专家来不及“反驳”错误，rebut it。(10)informed：形容词作定语，达成“有据/明智的”结论，informed conclusions。干扰项：remote(遥远的)无对应空；decline(下降)搭配不符；rigid(僵硬的)无合适语义位；costly(昂贵的)语义不合任何空且与sensational无近义冲突；sincere(真诚的)语义和槽位均不匹配，五项对每空均失败。
